skill.name: explore-run
skill.description: Rigor Improve / Rigor Explore run leaf skill for bounded exploratory evidence in deep learning research repositories. Use when the researcher explicitly authorizes exploratory runs such as small-subset validation, short-cycle guess-and-check, batch sweeps, idle-GPU search, or quick transfer-learning trials, with fair-comparison caveats and no-overclaim summaries in `explore_outputs/`. Do not use for end-to-end exploration orchestration on top of `current_research`, trusted baseline execution, conservative training verification, default routing, verified SOTA claims, or implicit experimentation.
skill.summary: Bounded exploratory run planner for deep learning research with budget-aware variant matrix generation and ranking.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 45 potential issues, but all are false positives after manual review. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings (5 locations) are triggered by benign words like 'default' and 'short' in documentation and argparse scripts — no cryptographic operations exist. The 34 'Ruby/shell backtick' findings are markdown code spans (e.g., `explore_outputs/`, `current_research`) used for formatting, not shell execution. The 6 'path traversal' findings are `../../` relative path references in markdown docs. The 'system reconnaissance' flag on execution-policy.md is a documentation 'Avoid' section. No prompt injection attempts detected. The scripts are legitimate Python utilities for generating budget-aware experiment variant matrices.

low_findings.title: False positive: markdown backticks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static scanner detected 34 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across SKILL.md and references/execution-policy.md. These are all markdown code spans used for formatting file paths and identifiers (e.g., `explore_outputs/`, `current_research`, `../../references/`). No actual shell execution or code injection vectors exist in these documentation files.

low_findings.title: False positive: relative path references flagged as path traversal
low_findings.description: Six instances of 'path traversal sequence' were detected in SKILL.md. These are `../../references/` relative path references in markdown documentation pointing to shared files in the parent directory structure. No filesystem traversal vulnerability exists.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptographic algorithm detection
low_findings.description: Five instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' were flagged in agents/openai.yaml, scripts/plan_variants.py, and SKILL.md. These are false positives triggered by words like 'default' and 'short' in text content. No cryptographic operations exist in any of the scanned files.

content.value_statement: Deep learning researchers need to run quick exploratory trials without overclaiming results. This skill generates budget-aware variant matrices with fair-comparison caveats, keeping exploratory evidence clearly separated from trusted baselines.
actual_capabilities: Generates budget-aware variant matrices from spec JSON files with configurable axes, subset sizes, and step limits
Ranks candidate runs using weighted scoring across cost, success rate, and expected gain factors
Prunes variants to respect max_variants and max_short_cycle_runs budget constraints
Produces output bundles including CHANGESET.md, SCIENTIFIC_CHANGELOG.md, COMPARABILITY_REPORT.md, and TOP_RUNS.md
Isolates exploratory experiment state from trusted baseline execution lanes
Labels all run results as bounded evidence with explicit fair-comparison caveats
limitations: Does not perform end-to-end exploration orchestration across the current_research workflow
Does not execute training jobs directly — hands off to minimal-run-and-audit or run-train skills
Does not promote exploratory results into the trusted baseline lane automatically
Does not support trusted baseline execution or conservative training verification

use_cases.title: Small-subset validation sweep
use_cases.description: Run a quick grid sweep over learning rate and batch size on a 5% data subset to identify promising candidates before committing GPU hours.

use_cases.title: Idle-GPU search
use_cases.description: Use available idle GPU time to explore transfer learning trial configurations with short run cycles, ranked by expected gain.
use_cases.target_user: Research engineer filling idle compute windows

use_cases.title: Fair-comparison ranking
use_cases.description: Generate a ranked candidate list with explicit comparability caveats for human review before promoting any result to the trusted baseline.
use_cases.target_user: Lab lead preparing results for team review

prompt_templates.title: Basic variant matrix generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a variant matrix for an exploratory sweep. Use axes: learning_rate [1e-3, 3e-4, 1e-4], batch_size [32, 64], subset_sizes [0.05, 0.1], and short_run_steps [500]. Apply max_variants=6 and rank by cost efficiency.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time use — getting a ranked candidate list

prompt_templates.title: Weighted scoring with custom factors
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an exploratory run prioritizing expected gain over cost. Set selection_weights to {expected_gain: 0.6, success_rate: 0.3, cost: 0.1}. Use axis: optimizer in [adamw, sgd, lion] with subset_size 0.1 and short_run_steps 1000.

prompt_templates.title: Transfer learning trial planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan quick transfer learning trials from a frozen backbone. Set variant_axes to {head_arch: [linear, mlp_2layer], dropout: [0.1, 0.3]}. Apply max_short_cycle_runs=3 and write outputs to explore_outputs/.

prompt_templates.title: Full pipeline with comparability report
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full explore-run pipeline: generate the variant matrix, apply budget pruning, and produce a COMPARABILITY_REPORT.md highlighting which pairs are not directly fair to compare. Label all results as bounded evidence.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert — end-to-end exploratory run with audit trail

output_examples.input: Spec with axes {lr: [1e-3, 1e-4], batch: [32]}, subset_sizes [0.1], short_run_steps [500], max_variants=2
output_examples.output: Generated matrix: 4 raw variants → 2 selected. Top: variant-001 (lr=1e-3, batch=32, total_score=0.782). Second: variant-003 (lr=1e-3, batch=32 swapped). Written to explore_outputs/CHANGESET.md and TOP_RUNS.md. Note: results labeled as bounded evidence — not promoted to trusted baseline.

output_examples.input: Spec with selection_weights={expected_gain: 0.7} and 6 raw variants across optimizer axis
output_examples.output: Reweighted ranking applied. Top 3 candidates favor aggressive optimizers with higher predicted gain scores. Written to explore_outputs/ with COMPARABILITY_REPORT.md flagging optimizer pair comparisons as not directly fair without matched step counts.

best_practices: Always set explicit max_variants and max_short_cycle_runs to bound compute usage before generating the matrix
Prefer small-subset and short-cycle checks first before committing to heavier exploratory runs
Review the COMPARABILITY_REPORT.md output before promoting any exploratory result to the trusted baseline
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for trusted baseline execution or conservative training verification
Do not promote exploratory results into the trusted lane automatically without human review
Do not use this skill as an end-to-end current_research exploration orchestrator — use ai-research-explore instead

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It plans bounded exploratory experiment runs in deep learning research, generating ranked variant matrices with fair-comparison caveats.

faq.question: Does it execute training jobs?
faq.answer: No. It plans and ranks candidates, then hands off actual execution to minimal-run-and-audit or run-train.

faq.question: Can it promote results to the trusted baseline?
faq.answer: No. Exploratory results stay isolated and must be reviewed by a human before promotion.

faq.question: What output files does it produce?
faq.answer: CHANGESET.md, SCIENTIFIC_CHANGELOG.md, COMPARABILITY_REPORT.md, TOP_RUNS.md, and status.json in explore_outputs/.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
